statement: Intel announced a EUR 5 billion (USD 5.7 billion) investment to expand its Leixlip, Ireland campus, adding Intel 3-node capacity for Xeon 6 and next-generation Xeon server/data-center processors.
why: The expansion adds non-GPU server-CPU and domestic foundry capacity, a modest increase in alternative compute supply and advanced-node manufacturing capacity relevant to the AI data-center supply chain.

impact.mechanism: New Intel 3 fab capacity for Xeon server CPUs expands non-GPU compute and foundry supply, a competitive/alternative-supply signal to merchant GPU.

evidence.excerpt: Investment explicitly tied to output of Intel Xeon 6 processors and next-generation Intel Xeon processors built on the Intel 3 node -- i.e. server/data-center supply capacity, not consumer GPU.

confidence.basis: Single source (Intel press release, labeled secondary tier); server-CPU capacity only, tangential to GPU; capex-share context unverified.
